topic.name: Renewable Fuel Policies
topic.description: Legislation and regulations aimed at promoting the use of renewable fuels, particularly ethanol, in transportation in the United States.

Document 1: eliminate the volume standards under the renewable fuel program applicable to corn-starch ethanol.
Document 2: requires each manufacturer to ensure that at least 50% of 2011 and 2012 model year automobiles and light duty trucks... are dual fueled.

Document 3: require the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to promulgate standards for mercury emissions from electric utility steam generating units.
Document 4: revise the Renewable Fuel Program to provide that the applicable volume of renewable fuel required in transportation fuel... shall apply in the aggregate to renewable fuel and domestic alternative fuel.
Document 5: requires each fleet of a manufacturer of passenger automobiles... to comprise at least 30% qualified vehicles in model year 2016.
Document 6: amends the Clean Air Act by revising the renewable fuel program, which requires transportation fuel to contain a minimum volume of renewable fuel.
Document 7: redefine the term 'cellulosic biomass ethanol' to mean ethanol derived from any cellulose, hemicellulose, or lignin that is derived from renewable biomass.

topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at enhancing accountability, transparency, and analysis in federal rulemaking processes.

topic.name: Telecommunications and Privacy Legislation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses legislation related to telecommunications regulation and the protection of personal privacy in digital communications.

Document 1: Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to exclude from the definition of 'common carrier' a provider of an information service.
Classifies broadband Internet access service as an 'information service' under such Act.
Document 2: Amends the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act of 1978 to limit the FBI's authority to seek business records concerning U.S. persons.
Removes the FBI's authority to make applications for investigations regarding a U.S. person.

Document 3: Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to make it unlawful to transmit misleading caller identification information.
Document 4: Prohibits data brokers from obtaining personal information through fraudulent means.
Requires data brokers to ensure the accuracy of personal information they maintain.
Document 5: Defines 'data broker' as a commercial entity that collects personal information for sale.
Requires data brokers to provide individuals a cost-free means to review their personal information.

topic.name: Consumer Credit Regulation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at protecting consumers from excessive fees and interest rates in credit transactions.

Document 1: prohibit a creditor from extending credit to a consumer under an open end consumer credit plan for which the fee and interest rate exceeds 36%
Empowers state Attorneys General to enforce this Act.
Document 3: Amends the Truth in Lending Act with respect to requirements for disclosure to a consumer of points and fees information
when the total points and fees the consumer must pay at or before closing will exceed 8% percent of the total loan amount or $400, whichever is greater.
Document 4: prohibit the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System and the Secretary of the Treasury from determining that real estate brokerage activity or real estate management activity is financial in nature
Exempts from such prohibition activities of a bank holding company that directly relate to managing any real property owned by national banks or their affiliates.

topic.name: Innovation in Development and Manufacturing
topic.description: This topic encompasses initiatives aimed at leveraging science, technology, and partnerships to drive innovation in both global development and the manufacturing sector.

supporting USAID Missions and Bureaus in applying science, technology, innovation, and partnership approaches

provide tools and training to aid the adoption of energy management systems

Document 7: increasing the application of science, technology, innovation and partnerships to develop and scale solutions to end extreme poverty

Provides reduced premiums for limited resource, beginning, and socially disadvantaged producers.

Document 5: Amends the Migratory Bird Treaty Act to allow an agricultural producer to take migratory game birds by the aid of baiting

Document 6: Amends the Migratory Bird Treaty Act to allow an agricultural producer to take migratory game birds by the aid of baiting
if the manipulation occurs on the producer's land

topic.name: Veterans Benefits and Support
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at improving burial benefits, survivor benefits, and healthcare coverage for veterans and their families.

Document 1: Increases the authorized allowance for burial and funeral expenses for deceased veterans
Increases the burial plot allowance for veterans who...
Document 2: Repeals certain provisions which require the offset of amounts paid in dependency and indemnity compensation
Prohibits recoupment by the Secretary of Defense of certain amounts previously paid to SBP recipients
Document 3: Prohibits terminating the eligibility of an individual who qualifies for treatment as a dependent under TRICARE
Requires the continuation of such coverage for the period beginning on the date of enactment of this Act

Document 7: Amends the Family and Medical Leave Act of 1993 to entitle an eligible employee who is the spouse, son, daughter, parent, or next of kin of a covered servicemember

topic.name: Mental Health Services for Older Adults
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving mental health services and support for older adults, including integrated care and substance abuse treatment.

Document 1: Amends the Public Health Service Act to require the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to make grants to public and private nonprofit entities to demonstrate ways of integrating mental health services for older adults into primary care settings.

Requires the Director of the Center for Mental Health Services to designate a Deputy Director for Older Adult Mental Health Services to develop and implement initiatives to address the mental health needs of older adults.
Document 2: Amends the Public Health Service Act to require the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to make grants to public and private nonprofit entities to demonstrate ways of integrating mental health services for older adults into primary care settings.

Directs the Secretary to give special consideration to providing treatment for older adults with substance abuse disorders when developing program priorities for addressing priority substance abuse treatment needs of regional and national significance.
Document 7: Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services acting through the Administrator of the Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration (SAMHSA), to award grants for demonstration projects to provide coordinated and integrated services to individuals with mental illnesses who have co-occurring primary care conditions and chronic diseases.

Requires the Secretary to make funds available for the development or expansion of programs to provide integrated treatment services for individuals with a serious mental illness and a co-occurring substance abuse disorder.

topic.name: Improving Educational Leadership and Literacy
topic.description: The documents focus on legislative efforts to enhance teaching quality, school leadership, and literacy education in K-12 settings.

Document 7: Directs the Secretary of Education to make grants to states, based on their proportion of poor children aged 5 to 17, to implement state middle grades needs analyses and improvement plans.
Defines 'eligible schools' as those where a high proportion of middle grade students matriculate to secondary schools with graduation rates below 65%.

topic.name: Land Trust and Management
topic.description: The documents discuss the designation, conveyance, and management of federal and non-federal lands, particularly in relation to Native American tribes and conservation areas.

Document 7: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to convey to Elko County, Nevada, without consideration, approximately 275 acres of land managed by the Bureau of Land Management (BLM).
Title II: Conveyance of Land to Indian Tribes - (Sec. 201) Holds in trust the following lands... for the Te-Moak Tribe of Western Shoshone Indians of Nevada.

topic.name: Small Business Administration Legislation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts aimed at enhancing support and opportunities for small businesses, particularly those owned by underserved and disadvantaged groups.

Document 1: Amends the Small Business Act to increase the maximum loan amount under the Small Business Administration (SBA) microloan program.
loans to start-up, newly established, or growing small businesses.
Document 2: Authorizes the Administrator of the Small Business Administration (SBA) to make $4 billion in guarantees of debentures of small business investment companies (SBICs).
Amends the Act to increase the maximum amount of outstanding leverage to be made available by the SBA.
Document 3: Establishes a three-year small business intermediary lending pilot program under which the Administrator of the Small Business Administration (SBA) may make direct loans to eligible nonprofit lending intermediaries.
Authorizes the Administrator to make one percent, 20-year loans of up to $3 million to up to 20 lending intermediaries.
Document 4: Amends the Small Business Act to make small businesses owned and controlled by service-disabled veterans eligible for small business development grants.
in the same manner as small businesses owned and controlled by socially and economically disadvantaged individuals.
Document 5: Amends the Small Business Act to make small businesses owned and controlled by service-disabled veterans eligible for small business development grants.
in the same manner as small businesses owned and controlled by socially and economically disadvantaged individuals.
Document 6: Amends the Small Business Act to authorize the Administrator of the Small Business Administration (SBA) to award contracts to small businesses owned and controlled by service-disabled veterans.
Requires annual certification to the SBA as to the service-disabled veteran-owned nature of the small business.
Document 7: Amends the Small Business Act with respect to the HUBZone program to authorize a contracting opportunity to be awarded under the program on the basis of competition restricted to qualified HUBZone small businesses.
Authorizes the Administrator of the Small Business Administration (SBA) to establish mentor-protege programs for small businesses.

Document 6: Reuniting Families Act - Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to establish the fiscal year worldwide level...

carrying a valid state license or permit for carrying a concealed firearm
Document 5: provide for reciprocity for the carrying of certain concealed firearms in different states
carrying a valid state license or permit for carrying a concealed firearm
Document 6: provide for reciprocity for the carrying of certain concealed firearms in states that allow their residents to carry such firearms

topic.name: Public Transportation and Local Government Funding
topic.description: Legislation aimed at providing financial assistance for public transportation and local government operations.

Document 1: Authorizes the Secretary of Transportation to make emergency grants to states and designated recipients for public transportation projects.
Requires amounts apportioned to a state or urbanized area to be used for operating expenses necessary to restore or prevent a reduction in public transportation service.
Document 2: Requires the Secretary of the Treasury to make entitlement payments to local governments.
Requires the use of such funds only for specified priority ordinary and necessary maintenance and operations.

topic.name: Government Accountability and Efficiency
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing the accountability, efficiency, and transparency of federal agencies and programs.

Document 1: Requires the Director of the Office of Management and Budget (OMB) to create a separate website about the reports issued by each Office of Inspector General.
A list of each federal agency that has an Office of Inspector General.
Document 2: Requires the Director of the Office of Management and Budget (OMB) to create a separate website about the reports issued by each Office of Inspector General.
A link to a website containing Inspector General reports.
Document 3: Establishes the Federal Agency Program Realignment and Closure Commission to evaluate each executive agency and its programs.
Submit to the President and Congress a list of the agencies and programs it recommends eliminating or realigning.
Document 4: Establishes the Federal Agency Program Realignment and Closure Commission to evaluate each executive agency and its programs.
Submit to the President and Congress a list of the agencies and programs it recommends eliminating or realigning.
Document 5: Requires the annual reports of federal agency chief financial officers to include the cost of multilingual services provided by federal agencies.
The Office of Management and Budget (OMB) to include in its financial status management reports a Multilingual Services Expense Report.
Document 6: Establishes the Federal Program Sunset Commission to evaluate the efficiency and public need for each federal program.
Submit to Congress and the President an annual report containing a list of federal programs that the Commission recommends to be abolished or reorganized.
Document 7: Establishes the Commission on Government Transformation to review work done by governmental and non-governmental entities.
Analyze organizational practices and management challenges of federal agencies.

topic.name: U.S. Defense Appropriations and Policies
topic.description: This topic encompasses the legislative measures and policies related to the funding and operational directives of the U.S. Department of Defense (DOD) for military construction, personnel deployment, and military operations.

Document 1: Appropriates to the Department of Defense (DOD) for FY2007 for military construction, family housing, and base realignment and closure functions.
an amount equal to that authorized to be appropriated for such purposes for such fiscal year under the Military Construction Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2007.
Document 2: Appropriates to the Department of Defense (DOD) for FY2007 for military construction, family housing, and base realignment and closure functions.
an amount equal to that authorized to be appropriated for such purposes for such fiscal year under the Military Construction Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2007.
Document 3: Prohibits any officer or employee of, or contractor or detailee to, the Central Intelligence Agency (CIA) from using an unmanned aerial vehicle to carry out a weapons strike or other lethal action.
Requires the President to transfer all authority to use such a vehicle to conduct such strikes or lethal action to the Department of Defense (DOD).
Document 4: Directs the Secretary of Defense (DOD) to ensure that no family member of an individual detained at Naval Station Guantanamo Bay, Cuba (Guantanamo), is permitted to visit such individual.
Includes as an individual detained at Guantanamo one located there on or after March 7, 2011, who: (1) is not a citizen of the United States or a member of the U.S. Armed Forces.
Document 5: Base Redevelopment and Indemnification Correction Act - Amends the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 1993 to expand the indemnification responsibilities of the Department of Defense (DOD).
to include all military installations closed since October 24, 1988.
Document 6: Directs the President to transition the mission of U.S. forces in Iraq to the following purposes: (1) conducting limited scope and duration operations against al Qaeda and other international terrorist organizations.
Prohibits funds from being obligated or expended to continue the U.S. Armed Forces deployment in Iraq as of 120 days after the enactment of this Act.
Document 7: Ensuring Military Readiness Through Stability and Predictability Deployment Policy Act - Prohibits any unit of the regular Armed Forces from being deployed for Operations Iraqi Freedom or Enduring Freedom unless the period between the most recent previous deployment and a subsequent deployment is equal to or longer than the period of the most recent previous deployment.
Expresses the sense of Congress that the optimal minimum period between such deployments should be equal to or longer than twice the period of the most recent previous deployment.

topic.name: Acrylic Fiber Tariff Amendments
topic.description: Documents discussing the temporary suspension of duties on various types of acrylic fibers and filament tow in the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on acrylic staple fibers containing at least 85 percent by weight of acrylonitrile units
average decitex of 1.9 (plus or minus 10 percent) and fiber length of 51 mm (plus or minus 10 percent)
Document 2: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on acrylic filament tow containing 85 percent or more by weight of acrylonitrile units
average decitex of 3.3 (plus or minus 10 percent) and an aggregate filament measure in the tow bundle between 660,000 and 1,2000,000 decitex
Document 3: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on acrylic staple fibers containing at least 85 percent by weight of acrylonitrile units
average decitex of 1.3 (plus or minus 10 percent) and fiber length of 38 mm (plus or minus 10 percent)
Document 4: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on acrylic staple fibers containing at least 85 percent by weight of acrylonitrile units
average decitex of 2.2 (plus or minus 10 percent) and fiber length of 38 mm (plus or minus 10 percent)
Document 5: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on acrylic filament tow containing 85 percent or more by weight of acrylonitrile units
average decitex of 4.1 (plus or minus 10 percent) and an aggregate filament measure in the tow bundle from 660,000 to 1,200,000 decitex
Document 6: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on acrylic fiber tow containing 85 percent or more by weight of acrylonitrile units
average decitex of 3.3 (plus or minus 10 percent) and an aggregate filament measure in the tow bundle from 660,000 to 1,200,000 decitex

topic.name: Regulation of Consumer Products and Pharmaceuticals
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at ensuring the safety and labeling of household products and pharmaceuticals to protect public health.

Document 1: Requires any household cleaning product or similar product to bear a label on the product's container or packaging that contains a complete and accurate list of all the product's ingredients.
Treats a product without such a label as a misbranded hazardous substance.
Document 2: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to require the Secretary of Health and Human Services to deny an application for a new animal drug that is a critical antimicrobial animal drug unless the applicant demonstrates that there is a reasonably certainty of no harm to human health.
Defines 'critical antimicrobial animal drug' as a drug intended for use in food-producing animals that contains specified antibiotics.
Document 3: Amends the Controlled Substances Act to provide that, in determining whether a controlled substance analogue was intended for human consumption, the following factors may be considered: the marketing, advertising, and labeling of the substance.

Declares that evidence that a substance was not marketed, advertised, or labeled for human consumption shall not by itself be sufficient to establish that the substance was not intended for human consumption.
Document 4: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to require the Secretary of Health and Human Services to deny an application for a new animal drug that is a critical antimicrobial animal drug unless the applicant demonstrates that there is a reasonably certainty of no harm to human health.

Requires the Secretary to withdraw approval of a nontherapeutic use of such drugs in food-producing animals two years after the date of enactment of this Act unless certain safety requirements are met.
Document 5: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (FFDCA) to revise provisions governing the importation of prescription drugs.
Prohibits the importation of a qualifying drug unless such drug is imported by: (1) a registered importer; or (2) an individual for personal use.
Document 6: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (FFDCA) to revise provisions governing the importation of prescription drugs.
Sets forth provisions governing the importation of qualifying drugs that are different from U.S. label drugs, including standards for judging such differences.
Document 7: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (FFDCA) to revise provisions governing the importation of prescription drugs.
States that the resale in the United States of prescription drugs that were properly sold abroad is not patent infringement.

topic.name: Energy Policy and Regulation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative acts related to energy production, specifically concerning oil and gas leasing and environmental protections.

Document 1: Approves a specified permit regarding certain energy-related facilities and land transportation crossings on the international boundaries of the United States for the Keystone XL pipeline project.
Prescribes permit requirements.
Document 3: Amends the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act to prohibit the conduct of oil and gas preleasing, leasing, and related activities in areas of the outer Continental Shelf located in the North Aleutian Basin Planning Area.
Amends the Energy Policy Act of 2005 to exclude such Planning Area from a mandated inventory of oil and natural gas resources beneath the waters of the outer Continental Shelf.
Document 4: Amends the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act to prohibit oil and gas preleasing, leasing, and related activities in areas of the Outer Continental Shelf located off the coast of California.

Amends the Energy Policy Act of 2005 to exclude such areas from the waters of the U.S. Outer Continental Shelf whose oil and natural gas resources the Secretary of Energy is required to inventory and analyze.
Document 7: Authorizes the Secretary of the Interior to accept bids on any new oil and gas leases of federal lands (including submerged lands) under the Mineral Leasing Act or the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act only from bidders certifying that all natural gas produced pursuant to such leases will be offered for sale only in the United States.

Amends the Mineral Leasing Act to prohibit granting a new right-of-way for a natural gas pipeline unless the right-of-way applicant certifies that all natural gas transported via such pipeline will be offered for sale only in the United States.

topic.name: Student Loan Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the regulation and management of student loans and education financing.

Document 1: amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965
require a student loan borrower's prepayment amount on a Federal Family Education Loan, Federal Direct Loan, or Federal Perkins Loan
Document 3: amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to allow borrowers that received loans under the William D. Ford Federal Direct Loan
to consolidate those loans as Federal Direct Consolidation Loans
Document 7: Amends the Truth in Lending Act to require lenders of private education loans to describe clearly and conspicuously, in writing, the cosigners' obligations regarding such loans
requires institutions of higher education to provide borrowers of federal education loans information at their entrance counseling

topic.name: Tax Code Amendments
topic.description: The documents discuss various amendments to the Internal Revenue Code aimed at revising tax treatments, credits, and liabilities for businesses and individuals.

Document 1: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to extend through 2007
Imposes a graduated increase in capital gains tax rates for any individual taxpayer whose adjusted gross income exceeds $1 million.
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow a qualified small business to elect to use a portion of its tax credit for increasing research expenditures
Allows an offset of research tax credit amounts against alternative minimum tax liability.
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to revise the tax treatment of S corporations
Permanently reducing from 10 to 5 years the period during which S corporation built-in gains are subject to tax.
Document 4: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to revise the tax treatment of S corporations
Permanently reducing from 10 to 7 years the period during which S corporation built-in gains are subject to tax.
Document 5: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to make permanent the tax credit for increasing research activities
Allow a qualified small business... to use a portion, up to $250,000 in a taxable year, of its tax credit for increasing research expenditures as an offset against its employment tax liability.
Document 6: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to revise the tax treatment of S corporations
Permanently reducing from 10 to 7 years the period during which S corporation built-in gains are subject to tax.
Document 7: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to reduce to 23% the maximum income tax rate for corporations
Make permanent the increased ($250,000) expensing allowance for depreciable business assets.
